heading: Omitted
label: 459a–4 Omitted
notes: CodificationSection, acts , ; , title I, § 105(a), , which transferred lands in Dare County, North Carolina, to the administrative jurisdiction of the Department of the Interior to be administered as a part of the Cape Hatteras National Seashore Recreational Area project, is omitted in view of , set out as a note under this section.June 3, 1948, ch. 39362 Stat. 301June 30, 1949, ch. 28863 Stat. 381Pub. L. 87–313

Disposal of Lands, , , provided:Pub. L. 87–31375 Stat. 675Sept. 26, 1961“That the tract of Federal property comprising eight and one-tenth acres of land situated in Dare County, North Carolina, approximately two miles north of Kitty Hawk, which was transferred to the administrative jurisdiction of the Department of the Interior by the Act of (; [this section]), to be administered as a part of the Cape Hatteras National Seashore Recreational Area, may be disposed of by the Administrator of General Services in accordance with the provisions of the Federal Property and Administrative Services Act of 1949, as amended [see chapters 1 to 11 of Title 40, Public Buildings, Property, and Works, and division C (except sections 3302, 3307(e), 3501(b), 3509, 3906, 4710, and 4711) of subtitle I of Title 41, Public Contracts].”June 3, 194862 Stat. 30116 U.S.C. 459a
